Common Cold.description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract, typically causing nasal symptoms, sore throat, and general malaise. Usually self-limiting within 7-10 days.

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.usage: Reduces fever and relieves headache, body pain, and sore throat
drugs.dosage: 500mg every 4-6 hours as needed, maximum 2000mg per day

drugs.name: Cetirizine
drugs.usage: Relieves runny nose, sneezing, and watery eyes

drugs.name: Oxymetazoline Nasal Spray
drugs.usage: Provides temporary relief from nasal congestion
drugs.dosage: 2-3 sprays per nostril twice daily, max 3 days

Influenza.description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses, characterized by sudden onset of fever, body aches, and respiratory symptoms. Can lead to serious complications.

drugs.name: Oseltamivir
drugs.usage: Antiviral treatment to reduce flu severity and duration
drugs.dosage: 75mg twice daily for 5 days, start within 48 hours of symptom onset

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.usage: Reduces fever, headache, and muscle/body pain
drugs.dosage: 400mg every 6-8 hours with food, max 1200mg per day

drugs.name: Guaifenesin
drugs.usage: Helps loosen mucus and relieve chest congestion
drugs.dosage: 200-400mg every 4 hours as needed, max 2400mg per day

COVID-19.description: A respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus. Symptoms range from mild to severe, with potential for loss of taste or smell being a distinguishing feature.

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.usage: Manages fever, headache, and body pain

drugs.name: Dexamethasone
drugs.usage: Reduces inflammation in moderate to severe cases (prescription required)
drugs.dosage: 6mg once daily for up to 10 days (hospitalized patients only)

drugs.name: Vitamin D3
drugs.usage: Supports immune function during recovery

Malaria.description: A life-threatening mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. Characterized by cyclical fever, chills, and sweating. Requires immediate medical treatment.

drugs.name: Artemether-Lumefantrine
drugs.usage: First-line antimalarial treatment for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria
drugs.dosage: Dosage based on body weight over 3 days (as per WHO guidelines)

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.usage: Manages fever and body aches associated with malaria

drugs.name: Oral Rehydration Salts
drugs.usage: Prevents dehydration from fever and sweating
drugs.dosage: 200-400ml after each loose stool or as needed

Dengue Fever.description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever, severe joint and muscle pain, and rash. Can progress to severe dengue (dengue hemorrhagic fever) which is life-threatening.

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.usage: First-line for fever and pain in dengue (AVOID aspirin/ibuprofen due to bleeding risk)

drugs.name: Oral Rehydration Salts
drugs.usage: Critical for maintaining hydration during febrile phase

drugs.name: Vitamin C
drugs.usage: Supports immune function and vascular integrity

Typhoid Fever.description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, transmitted through contaminated food and water. Causes sustained high fever, abdominal pain, and systemic symptoms.

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
drugs.usage: First-line antibiotic for typhoid fever in most regions

drugs.name: Azithromycin
drugs.usage: Alternative antibiotic for drug-resistant typhoid

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.usage: Manages fever and headache

Pneumonia.description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus. Causes cough, fever, chills, and difficulty breathing. Can be bacterial, viral, or fungal.

drugs.name: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate
drugs.usage: First-line antibiotic for community-acquired bacterial pneumonia
contraindications: History of cholestatic jaundice with this drug

drugs.name: Doxycycline
drugs.usage: Alternative antibiotic for atypical pneumonia

drugs.name: Salbutamol Inhaler
drugs.usage: Relieves bronchospasm and shortness of breath

Bronchitis.description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes, often following an upper respiratory infection. Characterized by persistent cough, mucus production, and chest discomfort. Usually viral and self-limiting.

drugs.name: Benzonatate
drugs.usage: Suppresses persistent cough

drugs.name: Guaifenesin
drugs.usage: Helps thin and loosen mucus for easier expulsion

drugs.name: Ambroxol
drugs.usage: Mucolytic agent that breaks down mucus structure

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, often caused by viral or bacterial infection. Leads to diarrhea, vomiting, abdominal pain, and dehydration risk.

drugs.name: Oral Rehydration Salts
drugs.usage: Most critical treatment to replace lost fluids and electrolytes

drugs.name: Ondansetron
drugs.usage: Anti-emetic to control nausea and vomiting

drugs.name: Zinc Supplement
drugs.usage: Reduces duration and severity of diarrhea

UTI.description: Urinary Tract Infection caused by bacteria (commonly E. coli) affecting the bladder or urethra. Causes painful urination, frequent urge to urinate, and lower abdominal discomfort.

drugs.name: Nitrofurantoin
drugs.usage: First-line antibiotic for uncomplicated lower UTI

drugs.name: Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole
drugs.usage: Alternative antibiotic for UTI

drugs.name: Phenazopyridine
drugs.usage: Urinary analgesic for pain relief during urination
drugs.dosage: 200mg three times daily for up to 2 days

Migraine.description: A neurological condition causing intense, throbbing headaches, often on one side of the head, accompanied by nausea, sensitivity to light and sound, and visual disturbances.

drugs.name: Sumatriptan
drugs.usage: First-line acute migraine treatment (triptan class)
drugs.dosage: 50-100mg at onset, may repeat after 2 hours, max 200mg per day

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.usage: Pain relief for mild to moderate migraine attacks
drugs.dosage: 400-600mg at onset, then 400mg every 6 hours, max 1200mg per day

drugs.name: Metoclopramide
drugs.usage: Anti-emetic for migraine-associated nausea, enhances analgesic absorption
drugs.dosage: 10mg at onset, may repeat every 8 hours

Hypertension.description: Persistently elevated blood pressure that increases risk of heart disease, stroke, and kidney damage. Often called the 'silent killer' as it may have no symptoms until organ damage occurs.

drugs.name: Amlodipine
drugs.usage: First-line calcium channel blocker for blood pressure control

drugs.name: Losartan
drugs.usage: Angiotensin II receptor blocker for blood pressure management

drugs.name: Hydrochlorothiazide
drugs.usage: Thiazide diuretic to reduce blood volume and blood pressure

Diabetes.description: A metabolic disorder characterized by elevated blood sugar levels due to insulin resistance or deficiency. Type 2 is the most common form, often associated with lifestyle factors.

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.usage: First-line medication for Type 2 diabetes, improves insulin sensitivity
drugs.dosage: 500mg twice daily with meals, titrate to max 2000mg/day

drugs.name: Glimepiride
drugs.usage: Stimulates insulin secretion from pancreatic beta cells

drugs.name: Sitagliptin
drugs.usage: DPP-4 inhibitor that increases incretin hormone levels

Anemia.description: A condition where the blood lacks adequate healthy red blood cells or hemoglobin, leading to reduced oxygen delivery to tissues. Causes fatigue, pallor, and shortness of breath.

drugs.name: Ferrous Sulfate
drugs.usage: Iron supplement to treat iron-deficiency anemia

drugs.name: Vitamin B12
drugs.usage: Treats megaloblastic anemia caused by B12 deficiency

drugs.name: Folic Acid
drugs.usage: Treats folate-deficiency anemia and supports red blood cell production
contraindications: Pernicious anemia (without B12, can mask neurological damage)

Allergic Rhinitis.description: An allergic response causing inflammation of the nasal passages, triggered by allergens such as pollen, dust mites, or pet dander. Causes sneezing, congestion, and itchy eyes.

drugs.name: Fluticasone Nasal Spray
drugs.usage: Topical corticosteroid for nasal inflammation and congestion

drugs.name: Cetirizine
drugs.usage: Second-generation antihistamine for sneezing, itching, and runny nose

drugs.name: Montelukast
drugs.usage: Leukotriene receptor antagonist for allergy symptoms

Sinusitis.description: Inflammation or infection of the paranasal sinuses, causing facial pain, nasal congestion, and discharge. Can be acute (viral/bacterial) or chronic.

drugs.name: Amoxicillin
drugs.usage: First-line antibiotic for bacterial sinusitis (if symptoms persist > 10 days)

drugs.name: Pseudoephedrine
drugs.usage: Oral decongestant to relieve nasal and sinus congestion

drugs.name: Fluticasone Nasal Spray
drugs.usage: Reduces sinus and nasal inflammation

Conjunctivitis.description: Inflammation of the conjunctiva (clear membrane lining the eyelid and eye), commonly called 'pink eye'. Can be viral, bacterial, or allergic in origin.

drugs.name: Chloramphenicol Eye Drops
drugs.usage: Broad-spectrum antibiotic for bacterial conjunctivitis
contraindications: History of blood dyscrasias with chloramphenicol

drugs.name: Olopatadine Eye Drops
drugs.usage: Antihistamine and mast cell stabilizer for allergic conjunctivitis

drugs.name: Artificial Tears
drugs.usage: Lubricates and soothes the eye surface
drugs.dosage: 1-2 drops as needed throughout the day

Appendicitis.description: Inflammation of the appendix, typically requiring emergency surgical intervention. Presents with periumbilical pain migrating to the right lower quadrant, nausea, and fever.

drugs.name: Ceftriaxone
drugs.usage: IV antibiotic for pre-operative coverage (hospital setting)

drugs.name: Metronidazole
drugs.usage: Covers anaerobic bacteria in abdominal infections (hospital setting)

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.usage: Pain and fever management while awaiting surgery

Kidney Stones.description: Hard deposits of minerals and salts that form inside the kidneys. Can cause severe flank pain, blood in urine, and urinary symptoms as stones pass through the urinary tract.

drugs.name: Tamsulosin
drugs.usage: Alpha-blocker that relaxes ureteral muscles to facilitate stone passage

drugs.name: Diclofenac
drugs.usage: Potent NSAID for renal colic pain relief
drugs.dosage: 50-75mg every 8 hours as needed, max 150mg per day

drugs.name: Potassium Citrate
drugs.usage: Alkalinizes urine to prevent certain types of stone recurrence

Asthma.description: A chronic respiratory condition characterized by airway inflammation and hyperresponsiveness, causing recurrent episodes of wheezing, breathlessness, and cough.

drugs.name: Salbutamol Inhaler
drugs.usage: Quick-relief bronchodilator for acute symptom relief
drugs.dosage: 2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed, or via nebulizer 2.5-5mg

drugs.name: Fluticasone Propionate Inhaler
drugs.usage: Inhaled corticosteroid for long-term asthma control

drugs.name: Montelukast
drugs.usage: Add-on therapy for asthma control, especially allergic asthma

Depression.description: A mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and loss of interest. Affects daily functioning and may involve physical symptoms like fatigue and sleep disturbance.

drugs.name: Sertraline
drugs.usage: First-line SSRI for major depressive disorder

drugs.name: Escitalopram
drugs.usage: SSRI with favorable side-effect profile for depression

drugs.name: Mirtazapine
drugs.usage: Atypical antidepressant, especially useful when insomnia and poor appetite are prominent

Anxiety Disorder.description: A mental health condition characterized by excessive worry, nervousness, and physical symptoms like rapid heartbeat, sweating, and tremor. Can significantly impair daily functioning.

drugs.name: Sertraline
drugs.usage: First-line SSRI for generalized anxiety disorder

drugs.name: Buspirone
drugs.usage: Non-benzodiazepine anxiolytic for chronic anxiety
drugs.dosage: 5mg two to three times daily, titrate to 15-30mg/day in divided doses

drugs.name: Propranolol
drugs.usage: Beta-blocker for physical symptoms of anxiety (rapid heartbeat, tremor)
drugs.dosage: 10-40mg as needed for situational anxiety, or 20-80mg/day for chronic use

Chickenpox.description: A highly contagious viral infection caused by varicella-zoster virus, characterized by an itchy rash with fluid-filled blisters, fever, and fatigue. Most common in children.

drugs.name: Calamine Lotion
drugs.usage: Topical anti-itch treatment for rash and blisters
drugs.dosage: Apply to affected areas as needed

drugs.name: Chlorpheniramine
drugs.usage: First-generation antihistamine to reduce itching and aid sleep

drugs.name: Paracetamol
drugs.usage: Fever and pain relief (AVOID ibuprofen/aspirin due to Reye syndrome risk)
drugs.dosage: 500mg every 4-6 hours, max 2000mg per day (children: dose by weight)
